habitat: Pelagic; breeds on coastal and offshore islands; forages in marine waters
story: The Streaked Shearwater (Calonectris leucomelas) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Procellariidae family. It is most commonly observed in Pelagic; breeds on coastal and offshore islands; forages in marine waters, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Near Threatened (NT) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Streaked Shearwater is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Streaked Shearwater into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
